channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US Secretary of State say Iran is in ‘a lot of trouble’
item.description: US Secretary of State say Iran is in ‘a lot of trouble’

item.title: Baulking at shipping sanctions, Greece balances Ukraine aid, fear of Russia
item.description: Sanctions on Russian LNG would hurt Greek shipping, argues Athens, but it also fears Moscow’s hybrid warfare.

item.title: Austria turns Adolf Hitler’s birthplace into police station
item.description: Austrian politicians hope the change will prevent neo-Nazis from visiting the building as a place of pilgrimage.

item.title: Lebanese army deployed in ‘pilot zone’ as families wait to return home
item.description: Lebanese army deployed in ‘pilot zone’ as families wait to return home

item.title: Graphic evidence shown as D4vd murder hearing begins
item.description: Hearing in singer D4vd's murder case begins.

item.title: Ukraine hits more warehouses of top Russian online retailer
item.description: Ukrainian drones have hit two more warehouses belonging to Russia’s biggest online retailer Wildberries.

item.title: America’s decades-old bipartisan consensus on Israel has been shattered
item.description: The Democratic establishment has faced growing pressure to change its stance on Israel. It has finally begun to do so.

item.title: Russia reports injuries after top online retailer Wildberries hit again
item.description: Company CEO says logistics centres in Krasnodar and Nevinnomyssk evacuated days after workers killed in earlier attack.

item.title: Ossoff challenges Hegseth on cost and duration of Iran war
item.description: Ossoff challenges Hegseth on cost and duration of Iran war

item.title: Al Jazeera reports from anti-US protest outside ASEAN summit
item.description: Al Jazeera reports from anti-US protest outside ASEAN summit

item.title: Iran, Gulf hit as Hormuz, Bab al-Mandeb close: What’s the latest?
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth tells US senators that the war has cost the US $37.5bn so far.

item.title: Mexico goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa retires after 22-year career
item.description: The 41-year-old, who played in six FIFA World Cups, announces he will retire from both club and country.

item.title: Chelsea signs Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa in record British deal
item.description: The 23-year-old Rogers, one of the Premier League's top attacking talents, has signed a contract until 2033.

item.title: Rabat demands answers after Moroccan man dies during arrest in Italy
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Meloni says truth must be 'pursued to the very end' after death of Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna.

item.title: FIFA World Cup final draws record viewership in United States
item.description: Figures reveal that viewership remained strong even after the USA were eliminated from tournament.

item.title: US ‘open’ to negotiations with Iran but Tehran not ‘serious’, Rubio says
item.description: US Secretary of State says Iranian control over Strait of Hormuz sets 'dangerous precedent', as attacks continue.

item.title: Air defences activate in Iran and flames rise from US attacks
item.description: Explosions could be heard over Tehran as the US army said it had completed its 11th consecutive day of strikes on Iran.

item.title: Algeria: Toxic colonisation
item.description: The French army’s illegal use of chemical weapons in Algeria’s war of independence, as revealed by testimony and documents.

item.title: India opposition leader Gandhi freed after detention at student-led protest
item.description: Demands from young people and Cockroach Janta Party pose one of the biggest challenges to PM Modi's government.

item.title: Iran toll in Hormuz would set ‘dangerous precedent’ says Rubio
item.description: Iran toll in Hormuz would set 'dangerous precedent' says Rubio

item.title: Can Pakistan-administered Kashmir’s election overcome a violent summer?
item.description: After months of protests and a crackdown, Pakistan-administered Kashmir heads into elections clouded by uncertainty.

item.title: Goats of football: Indian village holds tournament with an indigenous twist
item.description: In a remote district surrounded by forests and hills, teams from tribal villages compete for football glory - and goats.

item.title: US expands strikes on 11th straight night of attacks
item.description: The US military’s 11th consecutive night of strikes extended to Iranian provinces not previously hit.

item.title: Hegseth called a ‘failure’ over Iran as he reveals $37b cost of war
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gets into a heated exchange after being called a failed leader by Michigan Senator.

item.title: Guyana ferry capsize death toll rises to 53 as survivors describe terror
item.description: A ferry capsized in Guyana over the weekend, killing at least 53 people.
